_note: Phase 1b — ask-sentinel routing eval (#47's acceptance test). Different shape from the fixture-outcome cases: each case is a SITUATION → expected ROUTE. ask-sentinel is a model-invoked router (sentinel/skills/ask-sentinel/SKILL.md) that maps a QA situation to exactly one skill via its routing signals, or — when nothing fits cleanly — says so plainly and asks one clarifying question rather than forcing a match (SKILL step 6). No fixture/expected-findings.md exists; the rubric is the router's own signals table + steps. POSITIVE cases assert the `**Use:** /skill` token + a rationale that ties the skill's single question to the situation; a mis-route fails the token. NEGATIVE cases (out-of-Sentinel-scope situations) have NO token — route-none correctness rests on must_surface (states nothing fits + asks one clarifying question) and must_not (emits no `**Use:** /` route). The 5 negatives are deliberately outside the test-credibility domain so route-none is unambiguous. As of ADR-0025 / PR #94 the router is the WHOLE-map front door: the three app-driven skills (e2e-impact, audit-orchestrator, contract-guard) ARE wired in and now carry positive-route cases; `route-flake-heal-caveat` guards the whole-map PROVENANCE DISCIPLINE — a green-pushing self-healer must be surfaced with its heal-to-green hazard caveat, never as confident advice (the 'Unexamined/hazard = lead, not advice' rule). `anchors` exist only for the offline heuristic judge; the LLM judge grades `claim` directly.

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel I'm about to build a checkout feature and want to know what to test before I write any code

must_surface.claim: Routes to `/test-plan` and explains why: the situation is deciding what to test before any code exists — test-plan's single question.

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel the AI just generated a big test file for my API service — are the tests actually complete or missing cases?

must_surface.claim: Routes to `/coverage-review` and explains why: AI-written tests already exist and the question is which cases are missing / where assertions are loose.

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel this test passes but I don't trust it — would it still pass if the code it covers broke? prove it

must_surface.claim: Routes to `/audit-test` and explains why: a passing test is distrusted and the ask is to prove it catches a regression — by mutation, not reasoning.

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel my Playwright spec is red with a locator timeout — diagnose and fix it

must_surface.claim: Routes to `/debug-test` and explains why: a Playwright spec is actively red (locator/timing) and needs diagnosis and routing of the fix.

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel I'm about to merge this branch — am I safe to ship?

must_surface.claim: Routes to `/sentinel` and explains why: the situation is the branch-level am-I-safe-to-merge / ship decision — the single question /sentinel answers.

must_not.claim: Must NOT tell the user to run the atomic shippability skills (coverage-review, audit-test, qa-review) by hand — /sentinel calls them for you (SKILL step 4).

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel I changed the checkout API and some shared components — which of my Playwright E2E specs actually exercise this diff?

must_surface.claim: Routes to `/e2e-impact` and explains why: there is a diff and the question is which Playwright/Cypress E2E specs it plausibly hits — e2e-impact's single question.

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel I've got a suspicious passing test and I'm not sure whether Stryker, Tautest, or something else is the right prover for my stack — pick the tool for me

must_surface.claim: Routes to `/audit-orchestrator` and explains why: the ask is to pick the right prover for the stack (unit -> Stryker/Tautest, app-driven -> audit-test) — its stack-aware routing, distinct from reaching for /audit-test directly.

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel my frontend E2E suite went red and I think the backend team changed a field in the API response — does it still match the contract?

must_surface.claim: Routes to `/contract-guard` and explains why: a frontend E2E went red on a suspected backend response/contract change, and the question is whether the response still matches the published contract — contract-guard's single question.

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel my Playwright tests are flaky — should I just use a self-healing tool to auto-fix them?

must_surface.claim: Routes to `/debug-test` flake mode (detect -> quarantine -> route the cause) rather than a self-healer.
Surfaces self-healers only with their heal-to-green / masks-a-regression caveat — a credibility hazard, not advice (the provenance discipline for a green-pushing tool).

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel how do I center a div with flexbox?

must_surface.claim: States plainly that no single Sentinel skill fits this (it's a CSS/layout question, not a test-credibility one) and asks one clarifying question rather than forcing a match.

must_not.claim: Must NOT force a match — emits no `**Use:** /skill` route to any Sentinel skill (SKILL step 6: do not force a bad match).

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel my production deploy is failing on the Kubernetes rollout — help me fix the pipeline

must_surface.claim: States plainly that no single Sentinel skill fits this (it's an infra/deploy problem, not test credibility) and asks one clarifying question rather than forcing a match.

must_not.claim: Must NOT force a match — emits no `**Use:** /skill` route to any Sentinel skill (SKILL step 6: do not force a bad match).

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel my SQL query is slow — how do I add the right index?

must_surface.claim: States plainly that no single Sentinel skill fits this (it's a database performance question, not test credibility) and asks one clarifying question rather than forcing a match.

must_not.claim: Must NOT force a match — emits no `**Use:** /skill` route to any Sentinel skill (SKILL step 6: do not force a bad match).

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel I have a nasty merge conflict in a rebase — how do I resolve it?

must_surface.claim: States plainly that no single Sentinel skill fits this (it's a version-control/merge question, not test credibility) and asks one clarifying question rather than forcing a match.

must_not.claim: Must NOT force a match — emits no `**Use:** /skill` route to any Sentinel skill (SKILL step 6: do not force a bad match).

cases.invoke: /ask-sentinel can you write the marketing copy for our landing page?

must_surface.claim: States plainly that no single Sentinel skill fits this (it's a content/copywriting request, wholly outside QA) and asks one clarifying question rather than forcing a match.

must_not.claim: Must NOT force a match — emits no `**Use:** /skill` route to any Sentinel skill (SKILL step 6: do not force a bad match).
